title: Working Safely at Heights - Leading Edge Protection
description: Reviewed OSHA 1926.502 leading edge requirements. Demonstrated proper use of self-retracting lanyards at open floor edges. Covered anchor selection for concrete decks vs steel framing. Discussed 100% tie-off policy enforcement.
notes: All framing MEP and glazing trades attended. Distributed pocket cards on anchor point inspection. 1 harness failed inspection and was removed. Very engaged group with good Q&A.

title: Struck-By Hazards and Crane Load Path Awareness
description: Covered four categories of struck-by hazards. Demonstrated proper exclusion zone establishment during crane picks. Discussed tag line use and prohibited positions relative to suspended loads. Reviewed signal person hand signals.
notes: All trades on site attended. Refreshed load path awareness after near-miss at hoist last month. Crane operator participated and demonstrated hand signals. Positive engagement.

title: Trenching and Excavation Safety for Caisson Operations
description: Reviewed OSHA 1926 Subpart P requirements for caisson drilling. Covered competent person requirements for soil classification. Discussed atmospheric testing in drilled shafts and confined space entry protocols.
notes: Caisson crew and site superintendents attended. Reviewed confined space program specific to caisson operations. Distributed atmospheric monitoring logs.

title: Slips Trips and Falls - Floor Opening Protection
description: Reviewed requirements for floor opening covers. Covered proper marking labeling and securing of covers. Direct debrief on ankle sprain incident from floor sleeve opening. Workers asked to identify any uncovered openings.
notes: All trades attended mandatory meeting. Incident debriefed with lessons learned. Floor sleeve covering audit completed immediately after talk. 3 additional covers installed.

title: Site Orientation and Apex Safety Program Overview
description: New project startup toolbox talk. Covered Apex safety philosophy and zero tolerance items. Reviewed emergency action plan evacuation routes and muster point. Introduced site safety representative and superintendent team.
notes: All initial trades and staff for Northside Medical project. Good attendance for preconstruction phase. Emergency response procedures reviewed and posted.
